content: Population-level obesity interventions target multiple levels: (1) Policy interventions—sugar-sweetened beverage taxes (Mexico, Berkeley showing reduced consumption), front-of-package warning labels (Chile, Uruguay), marketing restrictions to children, school nutrition standards, urban planning for active transport; (2) Environmental changes—healthy food access initiatives, zoning limiting fast-food density near schools, safe parks and recreation facilities, workplace wellness programs; (3) Healthcare system approaches—routine BMI screening, intensive behavioral counseling (USPSTF recommendation), pharmacotherapy access (GLP-1 agonists showing 15-20% weight loss), bariatric surgery coverage; (4) Individual interventions—calorie restriction, increased physical activity, behavioral modification, but limited long-term success without environmental support. Evidence shows individual-focused interventions insufficient without addressing obesogenic environment. Weight stigma reduction essential for effective public health approach.
